Content_Sketch: Factory floor 3AM  -  sleeping bag between machines, phone glowing with notifications
Illustration_Note: Stickman lying in sleeping bag on factory floor, assembly line machines around, phone screen glowing blue

Content_Sketch: Phone screen: market crash ticker, rocket launch countdown, custody hearing reminder  -  all at once
Illustration_Note: Close-up of phone screen with multiple notification bubbles overlapping

Content_Sketch: You built all of this so you could leave
Illustration_Note: Wide shot: tiny stickman on factory floor, massive factory structure around, text overlay

Content_Sketch: You are seven. House in South Africa  -  large rooms, servants, father's mining money everywhere
Illustration_Note: Small stickman in large house interior, high ceilings, apartheid-era suburb visible through window

Content_Sketch: Reading encyclopedia alone for 10 hours  -  nobody interrupts because nobody notices
Illustration_Note: Tiny stickman sitting on floor surrounded by stacked books, clock showing time passing

Content_Sketch: Schoolyard  -  boys pin you down on concrete stairs. Broken nose. Hospitalized for two weeks.
Illustration_Note: Small stickman on ground at bottom of stairs, 3 faceless stickmen above, schoolyard background

Content_Sketch: Lying on bathroom floor looking at ceiling  -  first escape thought: I have to leave this place
Illustration_Note: Stickman lying on bathroom tile floor, looking up, bruise marks on face, ceiling light above

Content_Sketch: Age 12  -  taught yourself to code, sold a video game for $500. First proof: you can build your way out
Illustration_Note: Young stickman at computer screen, dollar signs floating, determined expression

Content_Sketch: Age 17  -  one suitcase, one-way ticket. Avoiding mandatory military service under apartheid. You don't say goodbye to your father.
Illustration_Note: Teen stickman at airport gate, single suitcase, airplane visible through window, South Africa map fading behind

Content_Sketch: You leave the country the way you'll leave everything that comes after  -  completely
Illustration_Note: Stickman walking away, South African landscape dissolving behind, text overlay

Content_Sketch: North America. YMCA showers. Friend's couch. Instant noodles for months.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in YMCA shower stall, then sleeping on couch, ramen cup on floor

Content_Sketch: First startup  -  rented office, two people, sleeping under the desk. You code all night.
Illustration_Note: Two stickmen in tiny office, one sleeping under desk, computer screens glowing

Content_Sketch: Company sells for $300M+. You are 27. You have more money than your father ever did.
Illustration_Note: Stickman looking at check/screen with large number, contrast with earlier poverty scenes

Content_Sketch: You pour everything  -  every dollar  -  into two new companies. Rocket company and electric car company. Simultaneously.
Illustration_Note: Split screen: rocket on launchpad left, car factory right, stickman in center pouring money into both

Content_Sketch: First marriage. Twins. Then triplets. Five children. You work 100 hours a week.
Illustration_Note: Stickman at desk with laptop, small stickmen children in background, wife stickman with arms crossed

Content_Sketch: Pattern: build, burn, build again. Leave South Africa. Leave the first company. Leave the marriage. The engine never changes.
Illustration_Note: Series of doorways  -  stickman walking through each one, each door closing behind

Content_Sketch: 2008. You are thirty-seven. Rocket has failed three times in a row.
Illustration_Note: Stickman standing before three crashed rocket debris piles, desert landscape

Content_Sketch: Car company is bleeding cash  -  dying week by week. Divorce happening at the same time. Assets frozen.
Illustration_Note: Stickman between two scenes: car factory with 'closing' sign, courtroom with gavel

Content_Sketch: You have enough money for exactly one more launch. If it fails, both companies die. You have nothing left.
Illustration_Note: Stickman looking at single rocket on pad, empty wallet/bank screen, desert sunset

Content_Sketch: You're not afraid of failure. You're afraid of stopping.
Illustration_Note: Close-up stickman face, dot eyes intense, rocket silhouette in background

Content_Sketch: Fourth launch. You watch from the control trailer. Screen flickers. Orbit confirmed.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in trailer watching monitors, green confirmation signal, engineers celebrating behind

Content_Sketch: Government contract arrives within weeks. Car company IPO  -  first automotive IPO since 1956.
Illustration_Note: Stickman at desk, two documents: government seal contract, stock exchange bell

Content_Sketch: Near-bankrupt to billionaire. Then richest person alive. $300B. The number means nothing to you. Or everything.
Illustration_Note: Stickman looking at phone with wealth ticker, expression unchanged, factory around him

Content_Sketch: Production hell. You sleep on the factory floor for months. 120 hours a week. Your body is a resource, not a person.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in sleeping bag on factory floor, assembly robots working around him, dark circles under eyes

Content_Sketch: You buy the biggest social media platform on earth. $44 billion. You carry a sink into the headquarters.
Illustration_Note: Stickman carrying sink into building, shocked faceless employees watching

Content_Sketch: Fire 80% of staff. Write code yourself. Advertisers flee. Platform value halves.
Illustration_Note: Stickman alone at desk in empty office, 'goodbye' boxes stacked, revenue chart dropping

Content_Sketch: You didn't buy it for money. You bought it for voice. The loudest microphone on earth.
Illustration_Note: Stickman at center of radiating signal waves, globe in background

Content_Sketch: Half the internet worships you. The other half wants you erased.
Illustration_Note: Split screen: crowd of stickmen cheering left, crowd of stickmen with protest signs right

Content_Sketch: Your eldest daughter changes her surname. Cuts contact publicly. Says she has no father.
Illustration_Note: Teen stickman turning away from SUBJECT, name tag being crossed out, distance between them

Content_Sketch: You respond on the platform you own. At two in the morning. To the whole world.
Illustration_Note: Phone screen showing tweet draft, timestamp 2:07 AM, empty room around

Content_Sketch: 14 children. 4 mothers. You can build a rocket that lands itself but you can't keep a family in the same room.
Illustration_Note: Stickman at center, scattered small stickmen in different directions, rocket landing perfectly in background

Content_Sketch: 2025. The president calls. He wants you inside the machine  -  cut waste, optimize the system.
Illustration_Note: Stickman on phone, White House silhouette on screen, transition from factory to government

Content_Sketch: Special government employee. No Senate confirmation. You walk into federal buildings like you walked into the factory  -  as if you own it.
Illustration_Note: Stickman with ID badge walking through security checkpoint, government corridor

Content_Sketch: Week one: access every budget database. Every contract. Every payment. You apply factory logic to a system built on inertia.
Illustration_Note: Stickman at multiple screens showing spreadsheets and databases, red highlights on waste items

Content_Sketch: Mass layoffs. Agency closures. You publish the data on the platform you own. The government's books  -  open to the world.
Illustration_Note: Stickman posting data on phone, government building with lights turning off, crowd reacting

Content_Sketch: A judge issues an injunction. You tweet about the ruling before your lawyers finish reading it.
Illustration_Note: Phone screen showing tweet about court ruling, notification from lawyer 'DO NOT POST' below

Content_Sketch: Conflict of interest: your companies hold billion-dollar contracts with the government you're reshaping. Nobody says it out loud. Everybody knows.
Illustration_Note: Split: stickman at government desk left, same stickman at rocket company desk right, money flowing between

Content_Sketch: Protesters outside your house. Your children's school needs security for the first time.
Illustration_Note: Stickman looking through window at crowd outside, small stickman children behind him, security guard at door

Content_Sketch: 130 days. Then you leave. Mission complete, you say. The system is unchanged. But you touched real power.
Illustration_Note: Stickman walking out of government building, briefing folder dropping into trash, looking back once

Content_Sketch: You didn't reform the government. You proved you could. That was always the point.
Illustration_Note: Stickman standing between factory and government building, both doors open, choosing neither

Content_Sketch: 3AM. April 2026. Launch site. The trailer smells like instant coffee and engine grease.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in trailer, coffee cup, monitors showing rocket telemetry, launchpad visible through window

Content_Sketch: Largest rocket in history on the pad. Your rocket company merged with your AI company  -  $1.25 trillion combined. More than the GDP of most countries.
Illustration_Note: Massive rocket silhouette on launchpad, stickman small in foreground, trillion-dollar number floating

Content_Sketch: Car company stopped making the flagship models. Pivoting to humanoid robots. Rocket company filing for IPO. Two trillion-dollar companies at once.
Illustration_Note: Split: robot assembly line left, stock exchange ticker right, stickman at center on phone

Content_Sketch: 847 unread messages. 6 companies. 14 children. You read none of them.
Illustration_Note: Phone screen showing 847 notification badge, finger not tapping, sleeping bag visible below

Content_Sketch: Your daughter talks about you in the press. Under her new name. Not yours.
Illustration_Note: Stickman reading phone, article headline blurred, expression still, alone in trailer

Content_Sketch: Your mother calls. You watch the phone ring. You don't pick up.
Illustration_Note: Phone screen showing 'Mom' calling, stickman's hand near phone but not picking up, sleeping bag

Content_Sketch: You look at the rocket through the trailer window. Mars window opens late 2026. Miss it and you wait two more years.
Illustration_Note: Stickman at trailer window, massive rocket lit by floodlights, red Mars visible in night sky

Content_Sketch: On paper you've won every game on earth. Richest person. Most powerful platform. Rockets that land themselves.
Illustration_Note: Stickman surrounded by achievements: rocket, car, platform logo, government badge  -  all floating

Content_Sketch: You escaped South Africa. Escaped poverty. Escaped marriages. Escaped gravity itself.
Illustration_Note: Sequence: South Africa fading → poverty fading → marriage fading → Earth fading → Mars appearing

Content_Sketch: Maybe escape is the only thing you actually know how to do.
Illustration_Note: Stickman alone, looking up at stars, sleeping bag around shoulders

Content_Sketch: Trailer. Sleeping bag. Phone on mute. Same position as lying on the bathroom floor at age twelve.
Illustration_Note: Stickman curled in sleeping bag in trailer, ghost image of child on bathroom floor overlaid

Content_Sketch: The boy who had to leave became the man who couldn't stop leaving.
Illustration_Note: Stickman walking through infinite doorways, each one larger than the last

Content_Sketch: You're still trying to leave. Only now there's nowhere left to go.
Illustration_Note: Stickman standing on launchpad looking up at empty sky, rocket behind, Earth below
